title: US, European stocks fall as Iran war drives oil rally, bond selloff
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: US and European stocks declined as tensions with Iran escalated, driving oil prices up and triggering a bond selloff. The majority of US military sites in the M
excerpt: US and European stocks declined as tensions with Iran escalated, driving oil prices up and triggering a bond selloff. The majority of US military sites in the Middle East were damaged by Iranian strikes. This situation has heightened global financial market volatility.

content.introduction: US and European stocks declined as tensions with Iran escalated, driving oil prices up and triggering a bond selloff. The majority of US military sites in the Middle East were damaged by Iranian strikes. This situation has heightened global financial market volatility.

# Majority of US military sites in Middle East damaged by Iran. USBasesStrike vrtc thumb cln.png. At least 16 American military sites have been damaged in Iranian strikes, making up the majority of US positions in the Middle East, a new CNN investigation can reveal. Authorities say a 36-year-old man has been arrested in connection with what they described as a “racially motivated assault.” CNN’s Clarissa Ward reports. 119274_TrainInfant vrtc thumb cln.png. President Trump said that no one knows the status of talks with Iran aside from himself and a handful of others. CNN's David Culver boards the first commercial flight from US to Venezuela in nearly seven years, as two countries are restoring relationships following the capture of former leader Nicolas Maduro in January. Ukraine’s 3rd Army Corps released video of what they say shows the evacuation of a 77-year-old woman with the aid of a robot. 119246_Iranian Holiday clean vrtc thumb.jpg.
